transcript.text: This is a quick tour of my personal calendar feature on my site.
It's a cool way to look at your life.
For example, I'll show you how it works.
I have the 70s.
I haven't gotten around to writing an introduction here yet.
We have the 70s, 80s, 90s, 0s, and 10s.
And so, for example, the 10s, it's an entire decade on one page.
And you can jump from one year to the next, 2010 to 2011, 2011 to 2012, 2012 to 2013, 2013 to 2014.
So you can superimpose and be like, hmm, what was I doing this day, 2017?
Oh, that's what I was doing.
Or you could do it the next 10 years prior.
For example, I'm on, like, June 12th of 2013.
Well, what was I doing on June 12th of 2003?
Well, let's see.
Now, this will just take me to another page, but it's on the same site.
So you can put this in you on a certain page, on a place on that page, just like Wikipedia does.
And so this is what I was doing.
I was with Paula, living at Capiana Barsua.
But I don't have a journal entry that day because I do have, I have drop-downs.
I have a CSS script that makes it so that you can mouse over and you can have, like, a blog on the page.
So there you go.
And so I have every single, every single decade.
Now, if you wanted to, if you like the idea, all you have to do is connect, is, like, click on the O's on the page.
And then it's all blank.
It's a blank page that you can fill in.
And so all you have to do is right-click, view source.
And then you, you know, Control-A, select all, and copy that, and then put that in the code view of your program, like Dreamweaver.
And then you have this page right here.
And all you have to do is, at that point, is you just click the background of this, of this, this thing is in the way,
And it's not getting out of the way, of this table, the cell.
And then make it a certain color to say that you were doing this that day, or the border of the cell, if you're doing two things on the same day.
And then as far as writing what's in the drop-down menu, you go into the code, and then between where it says span, you just write what you did.
And then it comes in on here.
So if you want to copy the CSS script, you would just right-click and copy the script here, because it's a,
And then you just put this script into the same folder as the pages, and then all, and then it's good to go.
All you have to do is look at the thing, and I use it like a WYSIWYG, Dreamweaver, real easy.
You don't have to know how to be a web designer to do that.
If you are interested in this, though, you do want to copy this and have extra ones of these laying around,
And then save them all the time, upload them with Dreamweaver.
It's Control-Out, it's Control-Shift-U, and upload it all the time in case you screw it up,
And you don't know how to fix it, you know, you can just download the one that you already uploaded.
So anyways, that's that.
